units.verbatim_text: But it's not about you, it's about my grace that will cover you. And there's a scriptural reference for this because Paul said, I think, first Corinthians chapter 9. I wrote this down, sorry, this is my first time. But Paul said in first Corinthians 15:19, he said, for I am the least of the Apostles. I am not worthy to be called an apostle because I persecuted the Church of God. But by the grace of God, I am what I am.
units.context: [Banky emphasizes that his ability to speak is due to God's grace, not his own qualifications.]

units.verbatim_text: So what that means to be my interpretation of that and my belief of what faith is, faith is believing that God can, trusting that he will, and then acting like it. So if you don't take anything from what I say today, it's that faith is believing that God can, trusting that he will, and then acting like it. Now, you know, at the point of salvation, you need faith, right, to receive Jesus Christ as your Lord and Savior because the Bible says by grace we are saved through faith.
units.context: [Banky defines faith as believing, trusting, and acting on God's promises.]

units.verbatim_text: And so I went to my mom one day and I said, why did you add blue to my name? For people that don't understand, yerba means God will help me build my house. And so I went to my mom and I said, well, why did you add only? Why didn't she just call me what my father was called? And she said, it's because we wanted to speak prophetically over your life that it is God that will build your house, that it is God that your help will come from.
units.context: [Banky shares how his name was chosen to reflect a prophetic belief in God's provision.]

units.verbatim_text: And I will never forget all through my years as a child, one of the number one prayer requests was that God would bless our family with our own home. And we prayed it every morning and every night. So in my mind as a child, owning a home became the symbol that we are successful or safe or secure because we prayed about it every day.
units.context: [Banky recalls his family's persistent prayers for a home, symbolizing security and success.]

units.verbatim_text: And I said, okay, is the restaurant good? Is this McDonald's that I'm going to do? So I worked at McDonald's for two years and then I moved on and I did other things. And, you know, so that was my life. So I finished from McDonald's, I went to university, I was studying and all of that good stuff. And eventually I made it through school, you know, God blessed me with scholarships and loans and things of that nature.
units.context: [Banky narrates his early work experience at McDonald's and his educational journey.]

units.verbatim_text: And I said, you know, I'd like to move back to Nigeria and face my music full-time. My parents almost lost their minds. I know it's different now because the music business and the movie business now has shown signs of life. We praise God for people like Kemi Adetiba that opened the door for us. Well, you know, there was a time that you cannot go to a Nigerian parent and say you want to do what?
units.context: [Banky discusses his decision to pursue music full-time and his parents' initial reaction.]

units.verbatim_text: And so I had that conversation with my dad and I said, you know, I need to know that I tried. I need to just follow my heart and follow what I've always been dreaming about. I just need to get it out of my system that I've tried. And if I go for a year and a half and it doesn't work, I'll leave it. I'll come back to America, I'll get a master's, I'll get another job, no problem.
units.context: [Banky explains his need to pursue his dreams despite potential failure.]

units.verbatim_text: And so, you know, I started making music again and we recorded a new album. It started doing well, you know, they started actually calling me to perform myself, making some small money. And any time I would make money, I would reinvest it in the business. So immediately I got to a place of success, people can bear me witness. I took anything that I would make and I found artists to sign and then I would invest it in them.
units.context: [Banky describes reinvesting his earnings into his music business and supporting other artists.]
